description: DEC-50 / FGAP-028: make action generation RESPONSIBILITY-MAP-driven so the plan produces a school-wide spread of DISTINCT actions per division purview, not the single-method-process concentration. (A) Add a LEAN grounding section `division_responsibility_summary` (division-grain responsibilities only, ~9.6KB vs the 60KB full atom inventory) in ai/services/grounding.py + register + test; add it to spec 05's grounding_sections (NOT the full atom inventory -- honor FGAP-022). (B) Rewrite spec 05 (snippet + fragment lockstep + content_hash re-stamp): the method becomes a process discipline (still required + type-valid + advisory), steps are generated FROM the division responsibility map (distinct action per relevant division, in/out-of-class incl. SAO/HRT/YGL/Dorm), dropping the one-step-per-template mandate (not parser-enforced). Parser/model/full-atom-inventory/production-templates untouched.
acceptance_criteria: The generated action set is a school-wide spread of DISTINCT actions: ownership (assignments) distributes across multiple divisions per their purview -- no single division owns a disproportionate majority of steps.
Each action is grounded in the responsible division's actual responsibilities (the responsibility map drives which division does what), not in one process method's template slots.

The plan completes the full 14-spec run (downstream specs 06-10 + the promotion gate work on the re-shaped step set) and stays within the stdout cap (no FGAP-022 regression -- spec 05's prompt does not re-balloon).

The existing qualities hold: in/out-of-class coverage (incl. SAO/HRT/YGL/Dorm), HOW-per-step, bilingual dual-purpose, standards key+name, verbatim labels; parser/catalogue-gate pass with no new fabrication flags.
Snippet<->substrate parity stays 14/14; the full project gate stays green.
